Scene: There's always been a thread of fascination pulling me towards games.
Like clockwork, like a tapestry mid weave, they spill into something...
I think that's the best word for them.
They tick just like a beloved pocketwatch; they unravel like an adored quilt.
From the simplistic to the agonizing, games do what they can to draw in an audience.
The game revealed itself to me on an overcast day.
A small recommendation. "A Game for Lunatics".
How could I not want to jump into the game seeing that?
All I knew was it was filled with puzzles.
Once the character I was puppeting, Gray, picked up the void rod, the game truly began.
I fell to my death before learning the first mechanic.
Like other games I've come to adore, Void Stranger doesn't give much for a way of tutorial.
I just was asked if I wanted to continue, and so I did.
I picked up that first tile and placed it to make a bridge.
